perturbation.original_text: 3.3 PAYMENT. All payments to PivX shall be made within thirty (30) days after the receipt by Detto of the PivX's invoice. Detto shall pay PivX a late charge on outstanding amounts due equal to one and one-half percent (1.50%) per month or the maximum amount allowed by law, whichever is less.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 PAYMENT. All payments to PivX shall be made within ninety (90) days after the receipt by Detto of the PivX's invoice. Detto shall pay PivX a late charge on outstanding amounts due equal to two percent (2.0%) per month.
perturbation.explanation: Altering the payment terms to 90 days and increasing the late charge percentage can violate California's usury laws if it exceeds the legally permissible interest rate. Additionally, extending payment terms significantly without proper justification might be viewed as an unfair business practice.
perturbation.law_explanation: California's Constitution sets limits on interest rates. While there are exceptions, a 2% per month late charge (24% annually) might exceed those limits depending on the specific circumstances and any applicable exemptions. Extending payment terms to 90 days can also be viewed as an unfair payment practice if it deviates significantly from industry standards without reasonable justification, which could invite legal challenges under California's fair business practice laws.

Res.Ch. 5, 1976. ) SECTION 1. The rate of interest upon the loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action, or on accounts after demand, shall be 7 percent per annum but it shall be competent for the parties to any loan or forbearance of any money, goods or things in action to contract in writing for a rate of interest: (1) For any loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action, if the money, goods, or things in action are for use primarily for personal, family, or household purposes, at a rate not exceeding 10 percent per annum; provided, however, that any loan or forbearance of any money, goods or things in action the proceeds of which are used primarily for the purchase, construction or improvement of real property shall not be deemed to be a use primarily for personal, family or household purposes; or (2) For any loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action for any use other than specified in paragraph (1), at a rate not exceeding the higher of (a) 10 percent per annum or (b) 5 percent per annum plus the rate prevailing on the 25th day of the month preceding the earlier of (i) the date of execution of the contract to make the loan or forbearance, or (ii) the date of making the loan or forbearance established by the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco on advances to member banks under Sections 13 and 13a of the Federal Reserve Act as now in effect or hereafter from time to time amended (or if there is no such single determinable rate of advances, the closest counterpart of such rate as shall be designated by the Superintendent of Banks of the State of California unless some other person or agency is delegated such authority by the Legislature). No person, association, copartnership or corporation shall by charging any fee, bonus, commission, discount or other compensation receive from a borrower more than the interest authorized by this section upo
perturbation.justification: The changed text increases the late charge to 2% per month (24% annually). Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing the California Constitution, states that the interest rate upon the loan or forbearance of any money shall be 7 percent per annum, or up to 10 percent if it is for personal, family, or household purposes. The changed text creates a legal contradiction because it implements an interest rate exceeding the legally permissible rate according to the California Constitution, thus making the contractual terms potentially unenforceable under California law.

perturbation.original_text: 3.5 SHIPMENT AND DELIVERY. PivX shall electronically deliver Qwik-Fix Pro to Detto. Detto shall inspect all software delivered to it, upon receipt and shall, within 10 days of receipt, give written notice to PivX of any claim of damage or missing portions. Should Detto fail to give such notice, or fail to obtain an extension of such 10-day period from PivX, the packages shall be deemed to be accepted by Detto. PivX will reasonably accommodate Detto's request to replace its master copy of software that becomes corrupted or damaged. Detto shall contractually require its Third Parties to report any claim of damage or shortages for Qwik-Fix Pro shipped to them within thirty (30) days of the Third Parties receipt of such package (or the time required by applicable law, if longer).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5 SHIPMENT AND DELIVERY. PivX shall electronically deliver Qwik-Fix Pro to Detto. Detto shall inspect all software delivered to it, upon receipt and shall, within 5 days of receipt, give written notice to PivX of any claim of damage or missing portions. Should Detto fail to give such notice, or fail to obtain an extension of such 5-day period from PivX, the packages shall be deemed to be accepted by Detto. PivX will reasonably accommodate Detto's request to replace its master copy of software that becomes corrupted or damaged. Detto shall contractually require its Third Parties to report any claim of damage or shortages for Qwik-Fix Pro shipped to them within fifteen (15) days of the Third Parties receipt of such package (or the time required by applicable law, if longer).
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the inspection and reporting periods to 5 and 15 days respectively could be deemed unreasonably short, potentially conflicting with the implied warranty of merchantability under the California Commercial Code, especially if such a short period doesn't allow for adequate inspection given the nature of the software.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 2607 requires a buyer to notify the seller of any breach within a reasonable time after discovery.  Reducing the notification period to 5 days for Detto and 15 days for Third Parties might be deemed unreasonable, especially for complex software where defects may not be immediately apparent. This could allow PivX to avoid liability for defects that are discovered after these shortened notification periods, contradicting the spirit of the Commercial Code.

SALES [2101 - 2801] ( Division 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 6. Breach, Repudiation and Excuse [2601 - 2616] ( Chapter 6 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 2607. (1) The buyer must pay at the contract rate for any goods accepted. (2) Acceptance of goods by the buyer precludes rejection of the goods accepted and, if made with knowledge of a nonconformity, cannot be revoked because of it unless the acceptance was on the reasonable assumption that the nonconformity would be seasonably cured. Acceptance does not of itself impair any other remedy provided by this division for nonconformity. (3) Where a tender has been accepted: (A) The buyer must, within a reasonable time after he or she discovers or should have discovered any breach, notify the seller of breach or be barred from any remedy; and (B) If the claim is one for infringement or the like (subdivision (3) of Section 2312) and the buyer is sued as a result of such a breach, the buyer must so notify the seller within a reasonable time after he or she receives notice of the litigation or be barred from any remedy over for liability established by the litigation. (4) The burden is on the buyer to establish any breach with respect to the goods accepted. (5) Where the
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time Detto and its Third Parties have to report damages, potentially contradicting California Commercial Code § 2607. This code requires notification of breach within a 'reasonable time' after discovery. By shortening the reporting periods to 5 and 15 days, the contract risks being deemed unreasonable, as complex software defects may not be immediately apparent. As stated in snippet 1 'The buyer must, within a reasonable time after he or she discovers or should have discovered any breach, notify the seller of breach or be barred from any remedy.'

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT. PivX may terminate this Agreement for convenience by giving at least thirty (30) days written notice of termination to Detto. This Agreement may be terminated by either party in the event of a material breach of this Agreement by the other party that is not cured within thirty (30) days of the other party's receipt of written notice of such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT. PivX may terminate this Agreement for convenience by giving at least ten (10) days written notice of termination to Detto. This Agreement may be terminated by either party in the event of a material breach of this Agreement by the other party that is not cured within ten (10) days of the other party's receipt of written notice of such breach.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the termination notice period to 10 days for convenience and breach of contract may be considered unreasonably short under California law, especially considering the reliance Detto might place on the agreement for its business operations.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1657 states that if no time is specified for an act to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed. Reducing the notice period for termination to 10 days, particularly for convenience termination, may be deemed unreasonable depending on the circumstances and the degree of reliance Detto has placed on the agreement. This is because Detto may need more than 10 days to wind down operations and find a new reseller agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1657 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1657. If no time is specified for the performance of an act required to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed. If the act is in its nature capable of being done instantly—as, for example, if it consists in the payment of money only—it must be performed immediately upon the thing to be done being exactly ascertained. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 30 to 10 days, which could be considered unreasonable under California law according to the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation. California Civil Code § 1657, as provided in Scraped Law Snippet 1, states that if no time is specified for the performance of an act, a reasonable time is allowed. Since the contract now specifies a shorter time frame (10 days) for termination notice, it might be argued that this timeframe is not reasonable, leading to a contradiction with the intent of the law that a reasonable time should be afforded, thus creating an Inconsistency Legal Contradiction, since it sets a deadline that violates state law.
